body.title: [sprint] ALARMS PANEL on the pages that bypass _html_wrap (chat SPA, dashboard, metrics)
body.intention: I intend that the ALARMS PANEL is truly on EVERY web page, including the main chat page and the dashboard/metrics pages that don't go through _html_wrap.
body.description:
**Problem:** T-system-alarms-web-panel injected the ALARMS PANEL via _html_wrap, covering the data/admin pages. Three pages bypass _html_wrap and currently show NO panel: (1) `/` the main chat page, served as a static built SPA (_DIST_DIR/index.html); (2) `/dashboard` (_DASHBOARD_HTML constant); (3) `/metrics-page` (_METRICS_HTML constant). The chat page is the one Akien looks at most — the loud channel must reach it.

**Shape:** Inject the ALARMS PANEL into _DASHBOARD_HTML and _METRICS_HTML (trivial — prepend the shared _ALARMS_PANEL constant inside <body>). For the SPA chat page, either (a) add the panel to the frontend source + rebuild the dist, or (b) inject server-side by templating the panel into the served index.html. Decide (a) vs (b) based on where the SPA source lives.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (_DASHBOARD_HTML, _METRICS_HTML); the SPA frontend source or the _index server path.
**Design rules:** reuse the existing _ALARMS_PANEL constant (one source of truth for the panel); zero new deps; conditional render preserved.
**Scope boundary:** IN — panel present on dashboard, metrics, and the chat SPA page. OUT — new panel features; the endpoint (shipped); admin close controls.
**Test plan:** _DASHBOARD_HTML and _METRICS_HTML contain id=sysalarm-panel; the served chat page contains it (test the chosen injection path).
**Completion criteria:** all three bypassing pages include the ALARMS PANEL markup; the panel still renders only when an alarm exists.

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
